exemplar.title: Does some knowledge belong only to particular communities of knowers?

detailedExemplarData.title: Does some knowledge belong only to particular communities of knowers?

strengths: Clear identification of three distinctive real-world objects with concrete contexts.
Consistent, well-developed links between each object and the IA prompt, supported by appropriate evidence and references.
Insightful engagement with TOK concepts (e.g., Kuhnâs question-selection, intergenerational transmission, medieval literacy) to justify exclusivity of knowledge.
weaknesses: Occasional drift into general commentary and minor lapses in focusing on explicit epistemic terminology.
Some metadata and contextual details (e.g., provenance dates, image captions) are missing, slightly weakening authenticity.
Citation format is inconsistent, and a few quotations are truncated or lack proper context, affecting readability.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exhibition meets the descriptors for the 7â8 band by clearly identifying three specific objects, situating them in precise real-world contexts, and providing strong, evidence-based explanations of how each object illustrates knowledge belonging to particular communities. The student effectively employs TOK concepts and references to support their points. Minor weaknessesâsuch as occasional general commentary, missing metadata, and inconsistent citation formattingâdo not detract from the overall coherence and strength of the arguments. On balance, the submission warrants the top mark within the 7â8 band, and thus a final score of 8 is justified.

annotations.comment: The opening prompt clearly frames the IA question but could be briefly contextualized by summarizing its significance to the chosen objects. A concise introductory sentence linking the prompt to the exhibitionâs focus on knowledge belonging to communities would strengthen coherence.

The student effectively labels Object 1 and specifies it is their fingernails, providing clarity. This direct identification aids readers in understanding the focus of the first example related to specific community expertise.

While the photograph visually supports the object, the student could enhance the context by indicating where and when the image was taken, reinforcing its authenticity and situating it concretely in the real world.

The descriptive opening explains nailâfiling technique but lacks an explicit link to the selected IA prompt. Including a statement such as âThis technique exemplifies knowledge held only by guitaristsâ would anchor the example to the TOK question.

The citation of Thomas Kuhnâs notion that âthe answers you get depend upon the questions you askâ is well integrated and demonstrates engagement with an epistemological theory to explain why nailâshaping knowledge remains communityâspecific.
The footnote formatting appears tangled (â<sup>1</sup>â). Ensuring correct superscript placement and clear reference numbers will improve professionalism and reader navigation.

The paragraph on cognitive constraints is insightful but doesnât tie directly to the IA prompt. The student should explicitly connect limited brain capacity to the idea that some knowledge is reserved for particular communities of knowers.

The concluding sentence reiterates the main point but is highly general. Adding specific examples of how nonâguitarists experience this gap in knowledge would deepen the justification for inclusion of the object.
To deepen analysis, the student could refer explicitly to an epistemic concept such as âtraditionâ or âauthorityâ and explain how it governs the recipeâs transmission and exclusivity.

The mention of intergenerational transmission is valid, but it lacks evidence (for example, a brief family anecdote or documented tradition) to substantiate the timeline and reinforce the objectâs significance.

Incorporating Russel M. Nelsonâs quote effectively highlights emotional and communal dimensions of the recipeâs knowledge, illustrating the subjective value conferred by specific communities of knowers.
The discussion on the value of exclusivity is broad. The student should clarify how restricting access preserves epistemic value, perhaps by linking to TOK ideas of scarcity or knowledge capital.
It would strengthen the argument to relate this point to a TOK concept like âknowledge as powerâ or âgatekeepingâ, demonstrating why exclusivity confers particular value in family contexts.
The summative statement is apt but could be more precise by explicitly naming the IA prompt and summarizing how the object directly addresses âknowledge belonging only to particular communitiesâ.
The image of the medieval manuscript clearly situates the object in its historical context, effectively demonstrating its tangible role in conveying knowledge within privileged communities.

The description of the Gratian Decretum gives requisite historical detail, illustrating the manuscriptâs original context. This bolsters the exhibitionâs demonstration of knowledge exclusivity in the Middle Ages.
The Chatfield quotation is truncated, ending with an ellipsis. Providing the full quotation or paraphrasing ensures clarity and strengthens the connection between mass literacy and the IA prompt.

The student could expand on how technological innovations like the printing press functioned as epistemic tools, transforming access and shifting the boundaries of who counts as a community of knowers.
Object 2 is clearly named and contextualized as a family recipe for droÅ¼dÅ¼Ã³wka, effectively signaling a distinct, realâworld object tied to heritage and communal knowledge.

The handwritten text visually demonstrates authenticity but lacks caption detail on provenance (e.g., date written, location). Adding this metadata would strengthen the realâworld context and connection to a knowersâ community.
The explanation that the recipe has been passed down through generations solidly demonstrates restricted access, supporting the claim that certain knowledge is confined within a family community.
Tracing the evolution from medieval literacy to modern mass literacy is insightful, demonstrating how shifts in access have changed the distribution of knowledge across communities.

The argument about change over time is compelling; however, linking this historical development explicitly to a TOK framework (e.g., âknowledge communitiesâ, âsharing mechanismsâ) would deepen the analysis.

The paragraph conflates access issues with intrinsic knowledge value. Clarify whether the focus is on absence of literacy skills or on the social mechanisms that restrict literacy to privileged groups.
The final sentence is abrupt and incomplete (âit.â). Rewriting to form a complete thought will improve coherence and effectively conclude the discussion of technological impact.
The Works Cited header is clear and distinct, signaling the transition to reference material and demonstrating organizational competency in academic conventions.
Citation entries vary in format (e.g., author names, article titles, URL placement). Adopting a single citation style (MLA, APA, or Chicago) throughout will enhance consistency and readability.
Grouping all online sources together and alphabetizing entries would improve navigability. Consider adding hanging indents and consistent access date formatting to conform to the chosen style guide.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
